changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "Satoko's Trap Course (Elementary)"

TextENGNew: "I can guarantee it. If Satoko holed up in these mountains, she could easily fend off an entire army division."
"I know the truth. Satoko was recruited by the special forces of some country to serve as their trap instructor."
"Stop saying weird stuff... Somebody help meee~~!!"
...How on earth did we end up like this? What traps had we stumbled into, and how?
Mion was stuck in an impossibly narrow pitfall she couldn't escape from, with only her head poking above ground.
Rena's head was completely covered by a tin bucket, and she was flailing around trying desperately to pull it off.
...Both situations were quite comical, but that was the least of our worries.@kAt least they had their feet on the ground.
"...Kei-chan, how are youuu? Think you can get free?@k@vS20/03/440300090.Hurry up and get yourself out so you can rescue this old man!"
"Y-You're the one who should be getting out and rescuing me, Mion! What exactly am I supposed to do wrapped up in this bamboo mat?!"
...My body was wound up tight in a bamboo mat roll, dangling about two meters off the ground.
Ugh... what kind of trap could possibly do this to a person?@kHuh?!@kSatoko!
The whole thing had started when everyone suggested that I, having grown up in the city, should get more acquainted with nature, and proposed a hike up the mountain.
"Hmm, but Rena doesn't really know the mountain out back either."
"Rena would probably get lost. Hau."
"The mountain behind the school, huh? ...I played there a lot as a kid, but... I haven't been there in years.@k@vS20/03/440300092.If you take a wrong turn out there, you can get seriously lost."
changeReason: Old translates 裏山 as 'mountain out back' (generic); new specifies 'mountain behind the school,' which adds specificity not necessarily in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: "Leave the mountain to us! The whole thing is our playground.@k@vS20/04/440400044.Right, Rika?"
"...Meep!"
"Oh! That's reassuring.@k@vS20/01/440100095.Then let's give these two the honor of being our guides and go for a hike!"
"But, Keiichi-kun... The summer vacation booklet says we shouldn't go play on the mountain because we'll get lost."
"...It isn't summer vacation yet, so it's fine. We know the paths, so there's nothing to worry about."
"The mountain has been our playground since forever! It's practically our backyard!@k@vS20/04/440400046.We know every shortcut and hidden path!"
...And indeed, Satoko and Rika-chan knew the trails like the backs of their hands.
Thanks to them, we got to take in the kind of pristine nature, sweeping vistas, and crisp mountain air you could never experience down in the village.
But then things started going horribly wrong!!
"Oh, yes. Everyone.@k@vS20/04/440400048.From here on, you must follow my footsteps exactly."
"Huh...? What's that all of a sudden?"
"...It would be in your best interest to do as Satoko says."
"Huh? Hey, Mii-chan, look at this.@k@vS20/02/440200062.What do you think it is?"
"Should I pull it? Should I?"
Ping.
changeReason: Japanese ピン is an onomatopoeia (a 'ping' sound); old translates it as 'Suddenly' (an adverb describing timing), while new uses 'Ping' which preserves the sound effect.

TextENGNew: ...Sproing.@k@|@y...CRASH!!!!
changeReason: Japanese びよよん is a springy/boing sound effect; old uses 'Squeak' while new uses 'Sproing,' which better captures the elastic snapping connotation.

TextENGNew: When Rena tugged on what looked like a piece of kite string, a bundle of bamboo spears lashed to a tree branch came crashing down right beside her!!
"Wahhh!! Wahhhh!!@k@vS20/03/440300094.What is this?!"
"What?!"
"What?!"
"Gyaaaa!!"
"...How nostalgic. If I recall correctly, Satoko made this trap during the second semester of second grade."
"That was a close call. The tips of those spears are smeared with dog droppings, so any wound would start to fester."
"W-When did this mountain turn into a guerrilla war zone?!"
...According to Satoko and Rika-chan... back in the early years of elementary school, Satoko had gone through a trap-making craze and had set them up all over the mountain...
"...Keiichi-kun, maybe... they didn't tell us to stay off the mountain because we'd get lost... but because it was dangerous...?"
Mion and I both nodded wholeheartedly at Rena's theory.
"Come now! Let us press onward.@k@vS20/04/440400051.If we don't hurry back, it'll get dark.@k@vS20/04/440400052.And once it gets dark, even I would be in danger."
S-So why did you bring us to this deathtrap of a mountain in the first place?!
Why does an ordinary, healthy Japanese boy like me have to worry about booby traps threatening his life?!
...And then...@kthe very instant Satoko and Rika-chan slipped out of sight, the three of us were promptly caught in traps of our own...
"...Someone get me out~~! I really need to use the bathroom~~!!"
"Never mind that~! Someone get this bucket off my head~~!!@k@vS20/02/440200066.I can't see anything~!@k@vS20/02/440200067.Hau~!!"
"...Rena, stop thrashing around so much. I... I can see your panties..."
Whap whap whap whap whap whap whack!!!
changeReason: Japanese スパパパパ is a rapid slapping/smacking sound; new 'Whap whap whap' better captures the repetitive slapping nature vs old 'Smash, crash' which implies heavier impacts.

TextENGNew: "O-Of course you can see... Even with a bucket on, you can definitely--"
"Gehah,"
"gohoh!!"
"Gbhhrgh!!"
Bucket-Rena was using me as a punching bag while Mion bawled her eyes out.
"...She can't get out of the pitfall? How sad, how sad."
changeReason: Old uses second person 'You' (speaking to Mion directly); new uses third person 'She' (speaking about Mion to others), changing who Rika is addressing.

TextENGNew: "Honestly, everyone! Didn't I warn you? I told you not to wander away from me!"
That's when it finally dawned on me.
...Satoko had been so strangely eager to take everyone up the mountain... because she wanted to show off all the traps she'd built over the years.
...You could have just shown them to us, you know.@k...I didn't need to experience them firsthand!!
Rika-chan patted each of our three heads in turn, looking thoroughly pleased with herself...
